channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Senate passes $70B immigration enforcement bill
item.description: The Senate passed legislation early Friday morning to fund President Trump's immigration enforcement agencies through the end of his term.
item.content:encoded: The Senate passed legislation early Friday morning to fund President Trump's immigration enforcement agencies through the end of his term.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: How Senate Republicans in their final months in office could affect Trump's agenda?
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin asks former Republican National Committee communications director Doug Heye how votes by outgoing Senate Republicans are likely to affect President Trump's agenda.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin asks former Republican National Committee communications director Doug Heye how votes by outgoing Senate Republicans are likely to affect President Trump's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: New World screwworm found in calf in Texas
item.description: A case of New World screwworm has been found in a calf in Texas. The flesh-eating fly, which was eradicated from the U.S. in the 1960s, poses a major threat to the cattle industry.
item.content:encoded: A case of New World screwworm has been found in a calf in Texas. The flesh-eating fly, which was eradicated from the U.S. in the 1960s, poses a major threat to the cattle industry.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Most K-12 teachers say AI's impact on education will eclipse the internet or computers
item.description: A new NPR/Ipsos poll shows many teachers are using AI to save time, but a majority are also worried the technology is making it harder for students to learn to think for themselves.
item.content:encoded: A new NPR/Ipsos poll shows many teachers are using AI to save time, but a majority are also worried the technology is making it harder for students to learn to think for themselves.
item.dc:creator: Lee V. Gaines

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Senate passes $70 billion immigration enforcement bill, Trump's agenda tests the limits of some lawmakers' support, John Bolton pleads guilty to mishandling classified information.
item.content:encoded: Senate passes $70 billion immigration enforcement bill, Trump's agenda tests the limits of some lawmakers' support, John Bolton pleads guilty to mishandling classified information.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Earth, Wind & Fire documentary explores band's creative genius
item.description: A new HBO documentary by Questlove tells the story of the R&B band Earth, Wind & Fire. Morning Edition host A Martinez speaks with band members Philip Bailey, Verdine White and Ralph Johnson.
item.content:encoded:
Morning Edition about the new HBO documentary Earth, Wind & Fire (To Be Celestial vs That's the Weight of the World)'/>

A new HBO documentary by Questlove tells the story of the R&B band Earth, Wind & Fire. Morning Edition host A Martinez speaks with band members Philip Bailey, Verdine White and Ralph Johnson.

(Image credit: Erika Aguilar)
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: On a VA medical campus, one veteran created an escape for others
item.description: Through years of controversy and delayed construction, one Iraq veteran has been rehabilitating a Japanese garden in the middle of the on the vast VA campus in West Los Angeles.
item.content:encoded: Through years of controversy and delayed construction, one Iraq veteran has been rehabilitating a Japanese garden in the middle of the on the vast VA campus in West Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence

item.title: The life cycle of a tax loophole
item.description: The Planet Money team traces the life of a tax loophole from creation, discovery, exploitation -- all the way to watching it get closed shut.
item.content:encoded: The Planet Money team traces the life of a tax loophole from creation, discovery, exploitation -- all the way to watching it get closed shut.
item.dc:creator: Nick Fountain

item.title: Trial underway for teen accused of fatally stabbing another teen at Texas track meet
item.description: A Black teen faces first-degree murder charges in a highly anticipated trial following the killing of a white teenager at a Frisco, Texas, track meet last year.
item.content:encoded: A Black teen faces first-degree murder charges in a highly anticipated trial following the killing of a white teenager at a Frisco, Texas, track meet last year.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Love

item.title: Trump's choices tests the limits of Republican lawmakers' support
item.description: President Trump continues to pursue very personal agenda items that are testing the limits of support from Republican members of Congress.
item.content:encoded: President Trump continues to pursue very personal agenda items that are testing the limits of support from Republican members of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez
